tasks.title: Implement Real-time Keypress Event Handling
tasks.description: Set up `readline.emitKeypressEvents` and `process.stdin.setRawMode(true)` to detect keypresses in real-time, guarding with `process.stdin.isTTY`.
tasks.details: Implement the necessary Node.js `readline` and `process.stdin` configurations to enable real-time keypress detection. Ensure `process.stdin.setRawMode(true)` is only called if `process.stdin.isTTY` is true to prevent errors in non-TTY environments (e.g., when running tests).
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that `keypress` events are emitted when keys are pressed. Test in both TTY and non-TTY environments (e.g., piping input) to ensure the TTY guard works correctly.

subtasks.title: Configure `readline` to Emit Keypress Events
subtasks.description: Set up the `readline` module to emit `keypress` events on `process.stdin`. This is the foundational step for real-time key detection.
subtasks.details: Call `readline.emitKeypressEvents(process.stdin)`. This should be done early in the application lifecycle where input handling is initialized.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that `process.stdin` starts emitting `keypress` events when a key is pressed (e.g., by attaching a temporary listener and logging the event data).

subtasks.title: Implement TTY Guard and Activate Raw Mode
subtasks.description: Conditionally enable raw mode for `process.stdin` only if the input stream is connected to a TTY, preventing errors in non-interactive environments.
subtasks.details: Add an `if (process.stdin.isTTY)` check. Inside this block, call `process.stdin.setRawMode(true)`. This ensures the application behaves correctly when piped input or run in a non-interactive script.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test by running the application normally (TTY) and then by piping input (e.g., `echo "test" | node app.js`) or redirecting from a file to ensure no errors occur in non-TTY environments and raw mode is not activated.

subtasks.title: Attach Generic `keypress` Event Listener
subtasks.description: Set up a basic event listener on `process.stdin` to capture all incoming `keypress` events, providing a hook for further processing.
subtasks.details: Implement `process.stdin.on('keypress', (chunk, key) => { /* placeholder for key processing */ });`. This listener will receive the `chunk` (the actual character) and `key` object (containing name, ctrl, meta, shift, sequence properties).
subtasks.testStrategy: Log the `chunk` and `key` objects received by the listener for various key presses (alphanumeric, special characters, Ctrl+C, arrow keys) to confirm correct event data is being captured.

subtasks.title: Implement Graceful Raw Mode Deactivation
subtasks.description: Ensure `process.stdin` is returned to its default, non-raw mode upon application exit or interruption, preventing the user's terminal from being left in an unusable state.
subtasks.details: Add event listeners for `process.on('exit')` and `process.on('SIGINT')` (for Ctrl+C). Inside these handlers, if `process.stdin.isTTY` is true and raw mode was activated, call `process.stdin.setRawMode(false)`. This is crucial for a good user experience.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the application, press Ctrl+C, and verify that the terminal prompt behaves normally afterward. Also, test by letting the application exit naturally (e.g., after a command) and confirm terminal state.

subtasks.title: Create a Dedicated Keypress Handler Module/Function
subtasks.description: Encapsulate all keypress event handling setup (configuration, TTY guard, listener attachment, and teardown) into a single, reusable function or module.
subtasks.details: Define a function, e.g., `initializeKeypressHandling()`, that orchestrates the calls to `readline.emitKeypressEvents`, the TTY check and `setRawMode(true)`, attaches the `keypress` listener, and sets up the exit handlers for `setRawMode(false)`. This function should be callable once at application startup.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that calling this single function correctly sets up all keypress handling without errors and that all individual components (raw mode, event emission, teardown) function as expected when the application runs and exits.

tasks.title: Define Command Catalogs for Chat and OSCE Modes
tasks.description: Create and maintain distinct data structures (e.g., arrays or objects) for Chat mode commands and OSCE mode commands as specified in the PRD.
tasks.details: Implement two separate lists of commands: one for Chat mode (help, exit, system status, health check, tutorial, start osce, session stats) and one for OSCE mode (score, progress, status, help, ?, case info, end case, exit osce, new case, restart, list, cases). These should be easily accessible by the suggestion rendering logic.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test to ensure both command catalogs contain the correct and complete list of commands as per the PRD.

subtasks.title: Design Command Catalog Data Structure
subtasks.description: Determine and define the optimal data structure (e.g., array of strings, array of objects) for storing both Chat and OSCE mode command catalogs, considering ease of access and potential future extensibility.
subtasks.details: Decide if a simple array of strings is sufficient or if a more complex structure (e.g., array of objects with properties like 'name', 'description', 'alias') is needed for future features, even if only 'name' is populated initially. This structure should facilitate easy iteration and lookup.
subtasks.testStrategy: N/A (design phase)

subtasks.title: Implement Chat Mode Command Catalog
subtasks.description: Create and populate the data structure for Chat mode commands with the specified list of commands.
subtasks.details: Populate the chosen data structure with the following commands: 'help', 'exit', 'system status', 'health check', 'tutorial', 'start osce', 'session stats'. Ensure the data structure adheres to the design established in subtask 1.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test to verify the Chat mode catalog contains exactly the specified commands and no others.

subtasks.title: Implement OSCE Mode Command Catalog
subtasks.description: Create and populate the data structure for OSCE mode commands with the specified list of commands.
subtasks.details: Populate the chosen data structure with the following commands: 'score', 'progress', 'status', 'help', '?', 'case info', 'end case', 'exit osce', 'new case', 'restart', 'list', 'cases'. Ensure the data structure adheres to the design established in subtask 1.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test to verify the OSCE mode catalog contains exactly the specified commands and no others.

subtasks.title: Expose Command Catalogs for Rendering Logic
subtasks.description: Make the implemented Chat and OSCE mode command catalogs easily accessible to the command suggestion rendering logic (Task 13).
subtasks.details: Implement a mechanism (e.g., module exports, a dedicated utility function, or a global constant if appropriate for the architecture) to provide the command catalogs to other parts of the application, specifically the suggestion rendering logic. This ensures they can be retrieved based on the current application mode.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test to confirm that the suggestion rendering logic can successfully retrieve the correct command catalog based on the current mode.

subtasks.title: Unit Test Command Catalog Content and Completeness
subtasks.description: Develop and execute comprehensive unit tests to ensure both Chat and OSCE mode command catalogs contain the correct and complete list of commands as per the PRD.
subtasks.details: Write unit tests that iterate through each catalog and assert that every expected command is present and correctly formatted, and that no unexpected commands are included. Verify the exact count of commands in each catalog against the PRD specification.
subtasks.testStrategy: Automated unit tests covering both Chat and OSCE command catalogs for content accuracy and completeness.

tasks.title: Implement Command Suggestion Rendering Logic
tasks.description: Develop a function responsible for formatting and printing a compact, readable list of commands, followed by re-displaying the current mode indicator.
tasks.details: This function should accept a list of commands and the current mode indicator string. It needs to print a short header, then each command in a compact format. Crucially, it must then re-display the current mode indicator (e.g., `Chat > ` or `OSCE > `) so the user's input prompt is restored.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test the rendering function with various command lists (empty, short, long) and different mode indicators to ensure correct formatting and prompt restoration.

subtasks.title: Define `renderCommandSuggestions` Function Signature
subtasks.description: Create the basic function structure, defining its parameters: `commands` (array of strings/objects) and `modeIndicator` (string).
subtasks.details: The function should be named `renderCommandSuggestions` (or similar) and accept `commands: string[]` and `modeIndicator: string`. This establishes the entry point for the rendering logic.
subtasks.testStrategy: N/A (This subtask focuses on function definition, not functional output.)

subtasks.title: Implement Header and Initial Output Formatting
subtasks.description: Add logic to print a short, informative header before the command list. This might also involve handling newlines to ensure the output starts cleanly.
subtasks.details: Print a header like 'Available Commands:' or similar. Ensure proper line breaks before and after the header to separate it from previous output and the command list, maintaining readability.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test that the correct header string is printed and followed by appropriate newlines, without any command data.

subtasks.title: Format and Print Command List Compactly
subtasks.description: Develop the logic to iterate through the provided list of commands and print them in a compact, readable format. This could involve multiple columns, comma-separated, or other space-saving techniques.
subtasks.details: Iterate over the `commands` array. For each command, format it concisely (e.g., `[command_name]`, or `command_name - description`). Consider using a fixed width or dynamic column layout if appropriate for 'compact'. Print each formatted command.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test with various command list sizes (empty, single, multiple, long names) to ensure compact and readable formatting. Verify output matches expected string for the command list section.

subtasks.title: Re-display Mode Indicator and Restore User Prompt
subtasks.description: Implement the crucial step of re-displaying the `modeIndicator` string to restore the user's input prompt after the suggestions have been printed.
subtasks.details: After printing the command list, print the `modeIndicator` string (e.g., `Chat > `) to the console. This is essential for the user to see their prompt again and continue typing. Ensure it appears on a new line or replaces the previous prompt line if necessary to restore the input prompt.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test that the exact mode indicator string is printed as the final output of the function, ensuring it's on a new line and correctly positioned for user input.

subtasks.title: Integrate and Unit Test Command Suggestion Rendering
subtasks.description: Integrate the developed function into the application flow and create comprehensive unit tests to verify its correct behavior, including various command list sizes (empty, short, long) and different mode indicators.
subtasks.details: Call the `renderCommandSuggestions` function from the appropriate part of the application (e.g., after a specific keypress). Write unit tests using a testing framework (e.g., Jest) to mock `console.log` and assert the entire output string matches expected formats for different inputs. Test cases should include: empty command list, single command, multiple commands, and different mode indicator strings.
subtasks.testStrategy: Comprehensive unit tests covering all aspects: header presence, command list formatting (empty, short, long), and final prompt restoration. Verify the entire output string from start to finish for various scenarios.

tasks.title: Integrate Keypress Detection with Suggestion Logic
tasks.description: Listen for the `/` keypress event and, upon detection, trigger the command suggestion rendering based on the current application mode.
tasks.details: Add an event listener for `process.stdin` `keypress` events. When the `name` property of the keypress event is `/` (and `sequence` is `/`), determine the current application mode (Chat or OSCE). Call the suggestion rendering function (from Task 13) with the appropriate command catalog (from Task 12). Ensure this does not interfere with `rl.on('line')`.
tasks.testStrategy: Manually test by running the app, switching modes, and typing `/` to verify correct suggestions appear for each mode. Ensure the prompt is restored and typing can continue.

subtasks.title: Implement Keypress Listener for Slash Command Trigger
subtasks.description: Set up an event listener on `process.stdin` to detect keypress events. This listener will be the entry point for the slash command logic.
subtasks.details: Add `process.stdin.on('keypress', (chunk, key) => { /* ... */ });` to capture real-time keypresses. This listener will be the primary handler for the slash command feature.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that the `keypress` event listener is correctly registered and fires when any key is pressed (e.g., by logging `chunk` and `key` within the handler).

subtasks.title: Filter and Validate Slash Keypress
subtasks.description: Within the keypress listener, implement logic to specifically identify the `/` keypress, ensuring it's the intended trigger for command suggestions.
subtasks.details: Inside the `keypress` event handler, add a conditional check: `if (key && key.name === '/' && key.sequence === '/') { /* ... trigger logic ... */ }`. This ensures only the specific slash key triggers the next steps.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manually test by typing various keys and then `/`. Verify that the subsequent logic (e.g., a console log) only executes when `/` is pressed, and not for other keys.

subtasks.title: Determine Current Application Mode
subtasks.description: Implement or integrate a mechanism to ascertain the current operational mode of the application (e.g., 'Chat' or 'OSCE') when the slash key is pressed.
subtasks.details: Introduce a function or access a global state variable, e.g., `getCurrentApplicationMode()`, which returns a string like 'Chat' or 'OSCE'. This function will be called immediately after the slash key is detected.
subtasks.testStrategy: Set the application to Chat mode, press `/`, and verify the determined mode is 'Chat'. Switch to OSCE mode, press `/`, and verify the determined mode is 'OSCE'.

subtasks.title: Select and Prepare Command Catalog
subtasks.description: Based on the determined application mode, retrieve the appropriate command catalog (Chat or OSCE) to be used for suggestion rendering.
subtasks.details: Using the mode obtained from subtask 14.3, select the correct command catalog (e.g., `chatCommandCatalog` or `osceCommandCatalog`) provided by Task 12. Store the selected catalog in a variable for the next step.
subtasks.testStrategy: After determining the mode, log the selected command catalog. Verify that the correct catalog (Chat or OSCE) is chosen based on the current application mode.

subtasks.title: Trigger Suggestion Rendering and Manage Prompt State
subtasks.description: Call the command suggestion rendering function (from Task 13) with the selected catalog and ensure that the `readline` prompt state is correctly managed afterward to prevent interference with `rl.on('line').`
subtasks.details: Invoke the suggestion rendering function, e.g., `renderSuggestions(selectedCatalog)`. Crucially, after rendering, manage the `readline` interface to ensure `rl.on('line')` continues to function. This may involve clearing the current line (`rl.write(null, { ctrl: true, name: 'u' })`), redrawing the prompt (`rl.prompt(true)`), or using `rl.write` to put the `/` back on the line if needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Press `/` in both Chat and OSCE modes. Verify that suggestions appear correctly. After suggestions are displayed, ensure the prompt is restored and you can continue typing normally, and `rl.on('line')` still captures your input.

tasks.title: Refactor and Integrate Slash Command Feature
tasks.description: Integrate all developed components (keypress handling, command catalogs, suggestion rendering, and keypress listener) cleanly into the main `app.js` CLI application.
tasks.details: Ensure the new real-time suggestion feature is seamlessly integrated without breaking existing `readline` input handling or `rl.on('line')` logic. Organize the code logically within `app.js` or related modules. Handle potential edge cases related to raw mode and TTY.
tasks.testStrategy: Perform end-to-end testing of the entire CLI application. Verify that all existing functionalities work as expected alongside the new slash command suggestions. Test switching modes and using regular commands.

subtasks.title: Integrate Core Keypress Listener and Suggestion Trigger
subtasks.description: Implement the `process.stdin` keypress listener within `app.js` or a new dedicated module. Configure it to detect the `/` keypress and, upon detection, call the suggestion rendering function (from Task 13) with the appropriate command catalog (from Task 12) based on the current application mode.
subtasks.details: This involves bringing the core logic from Task 14 into the main application flow. Ensure the listener is active when needed and inactive otherwise.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manually test by running the app, typing `/` in different modes (Chat, OSCE) to verify suggestions appear. Observe if the prompt is correctly restored.

subtasks.title: Refactor and Modularize Slash Command Logic
subtasks.description: Organize the integrated keypress handling, command catalog access, and suggestion rendering calls into well-defined functions or separate modules. Ensure `app.js` remains clean and primarily orchestrates the application flow.
subtasks.details: Create a dedicated module (e.g., `slashCommands.js`) to encapsulate the logic related to slash command detection, mode-based catalog selection, and calling the suggestion renderer. Export necessary functions for `app.js` to use.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that the application still functions correctly after refactoring. Ensure modularity by checking if `app.js` is cleaner and the new module is self-contained.

subtasks.title: Ensure `readline` and `rl.on('line')` Compatibility
subtasks.description: Address potential conflicts between the new keypress listener and existing `readline` input handling, specifically `rl.on('line')` logic. Implement mechanisms to ensure seamless operation, such as temporarily pausing `readline` or managing input streams during suggestion display.
subtasks.details: Investigate how `process.stdin` `keypress` events interact with `readline`'s internal buffer and prompt management. Implement logic to prevent duplicate input, ensure `rl.on('line')` receives clean input after suggestions, and that the prompt is correctly re-rendered.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test typing `/`, observing suggestions, then continuing to type a regular command and pressing Enter. Verify that `rl.on('line')` correctly processes the command and that no input is lost or duplicated.

subtasks.title: Implement Raw Mode and TTY Edge Case Handling
subtasks.description: Implement robust handling for `process.stdin` raw mode and TTY (Teletypewriter) specific behaviors to ensure the slash command feature works reliably across different terminal environments.
subtasks.details: Verify that `process.stdin.setRawMode(true)` is managed correctly, especially when switching between modes or when `readline` is active. Address issues like cursor positioning, line clearing, and prompt re-rendering that might behave differently in various TTY setups.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the feature in different terminal emulators (e.g., Git Bash, WSL, macOS Terminal) if possible. Verify behavior when `process.stdin.isTTY` is true/false.

subtasks.title: Conduct End-to-End Integration Testing and Initial Bug Fixing
subtasks.description: Perform comprehensive end-to-end testing of the entire CLI application to verify that all existing functionalities work as expected alongside the new real-time slash command suggestions. Identify and fix any immediate integration-related bugs.
subtasks.details: Execute a test plan covering: typing `/` in Chat mode, typing `/` in OSCE mode, switching modes and then typing `/`, typing regular commands after suggestions, and ensuring no existing features are broken. Document any found issues and implement fixes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Perform end-to-end testing of the entire CLI application. Verify that all existing functionalities work as expected alongside the new slash command suggestions. Test switching modes and using regular commands.

tasks.title: Verify MVP Acceptance Criteria and Document Feature
tasks.description: Conduct thorough testing against all MVP acceptance criteria and document the new real-time slash command suggestion feature.
tasks.details: Verify: Typing '/' in Chat mode displays Chat commands. Verify: Typing '/' in OSCE mode displays OSCE commands. Verify: After suggestions, the mode indicator is reprinted, and typing can continue normally. Document: Update relevant internal documentation or README with details on the new feature, how it works, and any known limitations (non-goals).
tasks.testStrategy: Execute a dedicated test plan covering all acceptance criteria. Record results.

subtasks.title: Set Up Test Environment and Review Test Plan
subtasks.description: Prepare the necessary environment for testing the real-time slash command suggestion feature and review the dedicated test plan covering all acceptance criteria.
subtasks.details: Ensure the application with the implemented feature is accessible and runnable for testing. Familiarize with the specific test cases outlined for verification.
subtasks.testStrategy: N/A (preparation step)

subtasks.title: Verify Chat Mode Command Display
subtasks.description: Execute test cases to confirm that typing '/' in Chat mode correctly displays the predefined Chat commands as suggestions.
subtasks.details: Focus on the accuracy and completeness of the displayed Chat commands as per the defined catalog.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manually or automatically input '/' in Chat mode and observe the output against the expected Chat command list. Record results.

subtasks.title: Verify OSCE Mode Command Display
subtasks.description: Execute test cases to confirm that typing '/' in OSCE mode correctly displays the predefined OSCE commands as suggestions.
subtasks.details: Focus on the accuracy and completeness of the displayed OSCE commands as per the defined catalog.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manually or automatically input '/' in OSCE mode and observe the output against the expected OSCE command list. Record results.

subtasks.title: Verify Post-Suggestion Mode Indicator Restoration
subtasks.description: Execute test cases to confirm that after command suggestions are displayed, the mode indicator is correctly reprinted, and normal typing functionality is restored.
subtasks.details: Test user experience after suggestions, ensuring the prompt is restored and further input is handled correctly without issues or visual glitches.
subtasks.testStrategy: After triggering suggestions in both Chat and OSCE modes, verify the prompt reappears and attempt to type further characters to ensure normal operation. Record results.

subtasks.title: Document Real-time Slash Command Feature
subtasks.description: Update relevant internal documentation (e.g., README, internal wiki) with details on the new real-time slash command suggestion feature, including its functionality, usage, and any known limitations or non-goals.
subtasks.details: Include how the feature works, examples of usage, and any identified limitations or non-goals discovered during testing or development.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review the updated documentation for accuracy, clarity, and completeness against the verified feature behavior.
